title: X-Forwarded-For trusted blindly for rate limiting
description: In server.rs:374-379, the inbox rate limiter keys on the first value from the `X-Forwarded-For` header, falling back to 'unknown'. This header is entirely attacker-controlled: any client can spoof it with an arbitrary IP address, completely bypassing the 60 req/min rate limit. An attacker can flood the inbox with malicious activities (e.g. mass Follow requests that trigger actor-document fetches) by rotating the X-Forwarded-For value on every request. Fix: rate limit on the TCP peer address (available via axum's `ConnectInfo` extractor), or at minimum validate that the X-Forwarded-For IP is in a known trusted proxy CIDR range before accepting it. If behind a trusted reverse proxy, add the peer IP to a proxy allowlist before trusting the header.
close_reason: P0 fixes: AtomicU64, UTF-8 path, OsRng, 410 cleanup, constant-time key compare, checked_mul, SSRF guard, sanitized URLs, persona exists check, UTF-8 safe truncate
